190.reason: [The condition `ch != '\\'` is incorrect. The variable `ch` holds the first character of the string (the opening quote), not the previous character. This condition should check if the character before the closing quote is not a backslash, but `ch` is always the opening quote, so it will always be true, causing the loop to break prematurely at the first double quote, even if it is escaped. The correct logic should track the previous character to handle escaped quotes.]

35.reason: [The program always closes both output files, but according to the problem description, only one of lexer.txt or error.txt should be generated depending on whether the input is correct or erroneous. Writing to both files and closing them unconditionally may cause incorrect output format or file content when an error occurs, because the problem requires outputting either tokens OR errors—not both. However, the more critical issue is that the loop continues even after an error token is found, and all tokens (including those after the error) are written to lexer.txt, which violates the specification that erroneous programs should ONLY produce error.txt.]
